summary: Crash in HandleDictionary.RegisterHandle (exception code 0x80000004) when creating SKFont or accessing SKSurface.Canvas on Windows 10 with SkiaSharp 2.80.2, affecting multiple users of an OpenGL-based game using RichTextKit for text rendering.

bugSignals.errorMessage: Exception Code 0x80000004 in HandleDictionary.RegisterHandle during SKFont construction and SKSurface.Canvas.get
bugSignals.stackTrace:
[Managed to Native Transition]
SkiaSharp.dll!SkiaSharp.HandleDictionary.RegisterHandle(System.IntPtr handle, SkiaSharp.SKObject instance)
SkiaSharp.dll!SkiaSharp.SKObject.RegisterHandle(System.IntPtr handle, SkiaSharp.SKObject instance)
SkiaSharp.dll!SkiaSharp.SKObject.Handle.set(System.IntPtr value)
SkiaSharp.dll!SkiaSharp.SKFont.SKFont(SkiaSharp.SKTypeface typeface, float size, float scaleX, float skewX)

stepsToReproduce: Use SkiaSharp 2.80.2 with RichTextKit in a game application on Windows 10
Render text-heavy UI using SKFont and SKSurface/SKCanvas
Crash occurs intermittently in HandleDictionary.RegisterHandle — more frequent on text-heavy screens
reproEvidence.environmentDetails: Windows 10 (10.0.19041/19042), .NET Framework 4.8 (CLR 4.8.4300.0/4.8.4341.0), SkiaSharp 2.80.2, OpenGL rendering context

repoLinks.description: RichTextKit library used by reporter for text rendering — its developer confirmed the issue

versionAnalysis.relevanceReason: The root cause — STA-alertable ReaderWriterLockSlim in HandleDictionary — was fixed in PR #1817 (merged Nov 2021) which replaced it with a non-alertable Win32 CRITICAL_SECTION. This fix shipped in SkiaSharp 2.88.x+.

fixStatus.reason: PR #1817 replaced the alertable ReaderWriterLockSlim with a non-alertable Win32 CRITICAL_SECTION on Windows, directly addressing the re-entrancy crash in HandleDictionary. The RichTextKit developer (toptensoftware) who commented on this issue also reported the same root cause in #1383.

analysis.summary: The crash in HandleDictionary.RegisterHandle is caused by STA thread re-entrancy during lock acquisition. On Windows with the STA threading model, .NET's ReaderWriterLockSlim.WaitOne() is alertable and can pump the Windows message loop (e.g., WM_PAINT), causing re-entrant calls into HandleDictionary while the lock is held. This was a known issue (#1383) fixed by PR #1817, which replaced the lock with a non-alertable Win32 CRITICAL_SECTION.
analysis.rationale: The crash occurs at the managed-to-native transition in HandleDictionary.RegisterHandle, with exception code 0x80000004 (STATUS_SINGLE_STEP trace trap). The RichTextKit developer (toptensoftware) commented on both this issue and #1383, confirming the same underlying problem. The PlatformLock.cs file explicitly documents this fix with a reference to #1383. The reporter uses version 2.80.2, which predates the fix in 2.88.x.

keySignals.interpretation: STATUS_SINGLE_STEP — trace trap caused by corrupted execution state from STA re-entrancy during lock wait.

keySignals.text: I've seen this in cases where OpenGL is not involved — just plain rendering to a Canvas
keySignals.interpretation: Confirms this is a HandleDictionary locking issue, not an OpenGL-specific problem.

keySignals.text: don't think this is threading related as all rendering in my case is done on a single UI thread
keySignals.interpretation: The STA re-entrancy issue manifests on a single UI thread — the lock wait pumps messages, causing re-entrant calls on the SAME thread.

keySignals.text: I just hit upon this issue and unfortunately this makes SkiaSharp unusable for my app
keySignals.interpretation: Same developer confirmed the same root cause on issue #1383, linking both reports.

codeInvestigation.finding: RegisterHandle acquires instancesLock.EnterWriteLock() — on Windows with STA, this uses ReaderWriterLockSlim which is alertable and pumps messages during wait, causing re-entrancy and crash.

Comment block explicitly documents the fix for #1383: '.NET locks are alertable when using the STA threading model and can cause the Windows message loop to be dispatched'. Solution replaces with Win32 CRITICAL_SECTION.
NonAlertableWin32Lock class uses Kernel32 InitializeCriticalSectionEx/EnterCriticalSection/LeaveCriticalSection — these are non-alertable and won't pump messages during wait.
SKFont constructor calls SkiaApi.sk_font_new_with_values which returns a handle, then the Handle setter calls RegisterHandle — this is the exact crash path from the stack trace.

resolution.hypothesis: STA-alertable lock in HandleDictionary causes re-entrancy via Windows message pump during lock acquisition. Fixed in PR #1817 with non-alertable Win32 CRITICAL_SECTION.

proposals.title: Upgrade to SkiaSharp 2.88.x or later
proposals.description: Upgrade from 2.80.2 to 2.88.4+ which includes the PlatformLock fix (PR #1817). This replaces the alertable ReaderWriterLockSlim with a non-alertable Win32 CRITICAL_SECTION on Windows, preventing the STA re-entrancy crash.

proposals.title: Set custom PlatformLock factory (for older versions)
proposals.description: If upgrading is not immediately possible, set PlatformLock.Factory to return a custom lock implementation that uses Win32 CRITICAL_SECTION or Monitor instead of ReaderWriterLockSlim. This must be done before any SkiaSharp calls.
proposals.codeSnippet:
// Must be called before any SkiaSharp usage
SkiaSharp.Internals.PlatformLock.Factory = () => new MyNonAlertableLock();

resolution.recommendedReason: The fix is already shipped in 2.88.4+. Upgrading is the simplest and most reliable solution. The PlatformLock.Factory workaround requires implementing a custom lock and may have edge cases.

actionability.reason: The crash is caused by STA-alertable locking in HandleDictionary, which was fixed in PR #1817 (merged Nov 2021) and shipped in SkiaSharp 2.88.4+. The RichTextKit developer confirmed the same root cause on #1383. The issue has been inactive since May 2021.

actions.description: Apply bug, core SkiaSharp, and Windows labels
Post analysis explaining the fix and suggesting upgrade
actions.comment:
Thanks for the detailed crash dumps and stack traces — they were very helpful in pinpointing the root cause.

This crash is caused by a known issue with the `HandleDictionary` lock on Windows. When using the STA threading model, .NET's `ReaderWriterLockSlim` is alertable and can pump the Windows message loop during lock waits, causing re-entrant calls into `HandleDictionary.RegisterHandle` — which corrupts state and triggers the crash (exception 0x80000004).

This was tracked as #1383 and fixed in PR #1817 (merged Nov 2021), which replaced the lock with a non-alertable Win32 `CRITICAL_SECTION`. The fix shipped in SkiaSharp **2.88.4+**.

Upgrading from 2.80.2 to 2.88.x (or the latest 3.x) should resolve this crash. If you're still seeing similar issues after upgrading, please let us know.
